rulings.Case Title: IN RE: AFFINIA DEFAULT SERVICES LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deposit Surplus Funds Pursuant To Civil Code 2924j And For Order Discharging Petitioner Declaration Of Nabeel M Zuberi
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2024 line 1. PETITIONER AFFINIA DEFAULT SERVICES LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DEPOSIT SURPLUS FUNDS PURSUANT TO CIVIL CODE 2924J AND FOR ORDER DISCHARGING PETITIONER DECLARATION OF NABEEL M ZUBERI is OFF CALENDAR. Dismissal on file. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: YING-TSUN SU VS. NATASHA V. ALEXEEVA ET AL

rulings.Case Title: LEANA PEREZ ET AL VS. BIANA CHERNOGUZ, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2024 line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT LEANA PEREZ, FRANCISCO ARMAS, SERGIO ARMAS, ANTHONY ARMAS, FRANCES ARMAS DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is continued to February 23, 2024. No later than February 9, 2024 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief/declaration, not to exceed 2 pages total (1) explaining the assertion in opposition that "cross-defendants failed to properly serve their first demurrer in a code-compliant manner," specifically identifying the defect in service; and (2) addressing whether Cross-Complaint responded to Cross-Defendants' meet and confer efforts (when and in what manner), of if there was no response, explaining why Cross-Complainant did not respond. Cross-Defendants may file a response to the supplemental brief/declaration, not to exceed 2 pages, no later than February 16, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SUZANNE SCHORNACK VS. PERRY BOUSSINA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16 C 1 For Reasonable Attorneys Fees Incurred In Bringing A Successful Anti-Slapp Motion
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT BRETT GIBBS, NILS ROSENQUEST NOTICE OF MOTION UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.16 C 1 FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEYS FEES INCURRED IN BRINGING A SUCCESSFUL ANTI-SLAPP MOTION is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and CCP 1013. The service by mailing was completed on January 4, 2024 according to the proof of service on file, which does not provide for 5 additional calendar days. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER, INC VS. LYLE POBLETE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2024 line 7. PLAINTIFF CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER, INC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Admissions are deemed admitted. Defendant shall provide complete verified responses without objections to Request for Production, Special Interrogatories and Form Interrogatories within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $560. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
